skill.name: gws-sheets
skill.description: Google Sheets CLI integration for reading, writing, and managing spreadsheet data programmatically.
skill.summary: Read and write Google Sheets spreadsheets from the command line using the official gws CLI tool.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation file for the official Google Workspace CLI (gws) tool. Detected patterns (external_commands, filesystem, network, weak_crypto) are triggered by legitimate CLI documentation syntax and Google Sheets API method names in documentation text, not actual dangerous code. No security concerns identified.

content.user_title: Manage Google Sheets from the command line
content.value_statement: Automate Google Sheets operations without leaving your terminal. Read data, append rows, and manage spreadsheets using simple CLI commands integrated with Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Read cell values and ranges from any Google Sheet
Append new rows of data to existing spreadsheets
Create new spreadsheets with specified properties
Batch update multiple cells or ranges in a single request
Filter and select specific data subsets using data filters
limitations: Requires pre-installed gws CLI tool and Google Workspace authentication
Cannot perform complex formulas or chart operations
Limited to operations supported by the Google Sheets API v4

use_cases.title: Data pipeline automation
use_cases.description: Automate data exports from scripts or databases into Google Sheets for team visibility.

use_cases.title: Spreadsheet reporting
use_cases.description: Generate reports by reading data from multiple sheets and aggregating results.

use_cases.title: Quick data lookups
use_cases.description: Retrieve specific values from shared team spreadsheets without opening a browser.

prompt_templates.title: Read sheet data
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gws to read values from spreadsheet [SPREADSHEET_ID], sheet 'Sheet1', range A1:D10
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to fetch specific data from a known spreadsheet

prompt_templates.title: Append new row
prompt_templates.prompt: Append a new row to spreadsheet [SPREADSHEET_ID] with values: [VALUES]
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to add data to the end of a sheet

prompt_templates.title: Create new spreadsheet
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Google Sheet titled '[TITLE]' and share the ID
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a new spreadsheet for a project

prompt_templates.title: Batch update cells
prompt_templates.prompt: Update spreadsheet [SPREADSHEET_ID] to set cell B2='Status', C2='Active'
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to modify multiple cells at once

output_examples.input: Read range A1:C3 from my team's budget spreadsheet
output_examples.output:
Retrieved 3 rows of data:
| Category | Budget | Spent |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Marketing | $5000 | $3200 |
| Engineering | $15000 | $12800 |

output_examples.input: Append a new row with user signup data
output_examples.output:
Successfully appended row to 'User Signups' sheet:
| Timestamp | Email | Plan |
|------------|-------|------|
| 2024-01-15 | user@example.com | Free |

best_practices: Always authenticate with gws before running commands to avoid permission errors
Use spreadsheet IDs instead of sharing URLs for more reliable access
Check available methods with 'gws schema sheets' before constructing complex queries
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode spreadsheet IDs in scripts that get committed to version control
Avoid making rapid sequential API calls without batching - this may hit rate limits
Do not assume sheet names match expected values - always verify sheet structure first

faq.question: What authentication does gws sheets require?
faq.answer: You need Google Workspace credentials configured with the gws CLI. Run 'gws auth login' to authenticate.

faq.question: How do I find a spreadsheet ID?
faq.answer: Open the spreadsheet in Google Sheets. The ID appears in the URL after '/d/' and before '/edit'.

faq.question: Can I use this to update formulas in cells?
faq.answer: Yes, you can set cell values including formulas using the values.update method.

faq.question: What is the rate limit for API calls?
faq.answer: Google Sheets API allows 100 read requests and 100 write requests per 100 seconds per project.

faq.question: Can I append multiple rows at once?
faq.answer: Yes, the append command accepts multiple values and will add them as consecutive rows.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Google personal accounts?
faq.answer: This skill works with any Google account that has access to Google Sheets API.
